concerns.text: T021 and T022 split 3DGS generation and inpainting into separate tasks without a single task explicitly orchestrating the end-to-end pipeline (degraded input → baseline → inpainted output) required to measure the improvement in SC-001. An implementer cannot deterministically produce the paired metrics without an explicit orchestration task.

FR-005 and SC-002 require a sensitivity analysis sweeping the Normalized Noise Fraction (NNF) to find a critical threshold. No task exists to generate the input dataset where NNF is systematically varied; T032 only analyzes static degradation levels. The implementer lacks the task to create the variable input data required for the sweep.

FR-001 mandates ensuring spatial alignment error < 2 meters and excluding samples that fail. T005 implements the transform logic, but no task explicitly implements the verification logic and automated exclusion of samples failing the < 2m threshold. The implementer has no task to enforce this core constraint.

FR-002 requires three distinct synthetic degradation modes: downscale, cloud mask, and temporal shifts. Tasks T014 and T015 only cover downscaling and cloud masks. There is no task to implement the 'temporal shifts' degradation mode, leaving a functional requirement unaddressed.

SC-003 requires measuring peak RAM and wall-clock time per scene against specific constraints. T023 handles OOM errors but does not include a task to instrument, capture, and log these specific performance metrics per scene. The implementer cannot produce the data required for SC-003 verification.

T011b acquires real cloud masks for validation (FR-006) but does not specify the task logic for the edge case where synthetic masks fail validation (similarity < 0.8). The implementer lacks a defined fallback path (e.g., switching to real masks) in the task list, creating ambiguity in execution.
